tu.prop: Whole-poem fallback because Chinese and English stanza structures do not align safely.

tuv.seg: 野有死麕，白茅包之，有女懷春，吉士誘之。 林有樸樕，野有死鹿，白茅純束，有女如玉。 舒而脫脫兮，無感我帨兮，無使尨也吠。

1 In the wild there is a dead antelope, And it is wrapped up wiih the white grass. Tliere is a youni^ lady with tliouglits natural to the spring, And a fine gCMitleinan would lead her astray.
2 In the forest there are the scrubby oaks ; In the wild there is a dead deer, And it is bound round with the white gras9. There is a young lady like a gem.
3 [She says], Slowly； gently, gently; Do not move my handkerchief; Do not make my dog bark. Flow great is that luxuriance, Those flowers of the sparrow-plum! The carriages of the kings daughter? Flow great is that luxuriance, The flowers like those of the peach-tree or the plum! [See] the grand-daughter of the tranquillizing king, And the son of the reverent marquis!
